I think that "buffer" is the margin of remaining context that if you impinge upon, it triggers the auto-compaction mechanism. This is a soft threshold that precedes the hard threshold of 1M tokens. I was suggesting that if you have autocompact turned on, they should lower the hard threshold.

reply_context.text:
They do this. See autocompact buffer.

However, it's not just the size of the compact prompt, the buffer also needs to have enough space the compaction output, which is probably the bigger chunk. So making the prompt a single token wouldn't help that much.

posts.text: My understanding of the physiology at play was that the CO2 only detaches from the heme group if there's another molecule that wants to bind more strongly, e.g. O2. Per WP, turns out CO2 doesn't bind to heme so doesn't even compete with O2. It's carried by amine groups on the Hgb molecule. TIL.

reply_context.text: But, aso worth noting... air is already 80% nitrogen. Consequently, nitrogen is the main way that CO2 gets carried out of our lungs anyway. It's only when there's no oxygen in it that its dangerous.

reply_context.text: citation needed. CO2 binds to hemoglobin. N2 does not. If N2 doesn't bind. there's nothing to displace the bound CO2 and it stays bound, accumulating in the blood.
